tokens: Houston, the problem, as you must know, with (parking the Rover at) 085 at 0.1 (as per checklist) is that such a reading puts us through a crater that's about 18, 20 feet deep, and it's too steep to climb in and out of.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: How far is it to the other side of it, John?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It's...Oh, it's only a couple of feet.
We'll get here at 085 at 100 meters.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Something on here has reset the range.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did you see the number on that?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, Charlie; understand you got the Muley rock and put it in the big rock bag?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Long Pause) And, John; once you get parked out there, we're going to need that whole front end brushed off.
If you just want to start on the panel and work back, that's probably the best way.
We'd like you to use the...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, we'd like you to use the small
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'm parked on a slope of about 10 degrees - or 5 or 6 or 7 degrees - (with the downslope) toward the lunar module, and it's my guess that this will help your cooling some, because it (meaning the Rover radiator)'s looking towards deep space a little.
And I'm about 100 yards (90 meters) directly aft of the lunar module.
Is that where you want this contraption to be?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And before you turn it off, there, we'd like a complete readout.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I don't want to do any unnecessary brushing.
How about somebody reading the EVA Closeout decal to me.
This thing is so dusty, I can't read anything.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That do it, Houston, for you?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: How about reading the Closeout decal to me there, because I can't see it for the dust.
Even after I brush it, I can't see it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Bus A and C should be Closed." (Pause)
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Tony; I've got two bags up, and I'm gonna have to wait and let John take in the rest, and I'm policing up the area, and it looks like everything (disposable) is pretty much under the LM.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, Auxiliary Circuit Breaker Bypass, On.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The Bypass switch is coming On.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let me line up the high gain.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, John, while you're up in the front end, take the TV lens shade off.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Just take it off and throw it away.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Before he hears Tony's reply) What do you want to do with it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, I tried some Max cooling there for a second, on this PLSS, and it really freezes you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Trying to line the Earth up.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: If you're out of things to do there, why don't you go on out where John is and see if you can find an igneous or a hard breccia to put on top of that LPM.
Use his camera to document it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I don't have a camera, though, now, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Verify that the circuit breakers you still have in on the panel are at Aux and circuit breakers A and Charlie (are Closed).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And if you have a chance to aim the high gain, we'll get our TV back.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Aux Power is in, Bus A is in, Bus B is in, and the rest of them are pulled.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: like B out, and Charlie in, and Delta out.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's the way I got it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A hard breccia or an igneous rock.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I can't do it from here, John.
Looks like to me you got to go right.
Or down, to you, I guess it is.
This one looks pretty good right here, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Do you want this rock brought back, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'd like you to document it before you touch it, and then when you get the LPM first measurement, we'll put it on the LPM, take a picture of it on the LPM, and then we'll get an LPM measurement of it, and then we'll sack it and bring it back.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It's a pretty good one, but it's too big to sack, unfortunately (Lost under Tony)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Do you have a smaller one around that we can get in the sack?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I tell you, when it gets to be a crescent Earth, that thing is really hard to see.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I going to tighten down your little screws there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: How about one a half-an-orange size, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm going to get a couple of cross-Sun stereos is all.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We've got the (sample) location on TV.
And, John, when you start dusting off the panel, we'd like to...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Laughs) It's just that you got a thermal leak there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It's an igneous rock, not a breccia.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And it's got that sugary texture to it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, why don't I do that, and you put the LPM out?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Why don't you set it on the seat, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Charlie, lay the camera up there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm not sure we got...We got a bag left, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I put a bag under the seat.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Here are the (LPM) power switches coming on, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Tony) I dusted the back mirror, and then I dusted the front, and now I got re-dust (the back mirror).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Charlie, we'd like you to dust that panel and the top of the console.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The control panel on the LRV.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Why do you want to do that, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We want to keep the temperature of the panel down.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Keep it nice for the next guy.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The top of the panel is dusted, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And we'll need all sides of that console and I guess that panel you just dusted.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The top and the driver's side is dusted.
I'm going to dust the LCRU.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Charlie, after dusting the LCRU there, you'll have to tear off one of those thermal blankets and put it over the control panel on the LCRU.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, John, we saw you get back and we started your clock.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) Let me show you what to do with that one, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Do you want a picture of it, don't you, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, it'd be a good idea.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I think I see how it goes.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You know, we sure hope you guys have enjoyed watching this as much as we've enjoyed doing it.
If there's one thing that's a real pleasure; it's this (one-sixth-)gravity environment.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And you've got a minute (on the LPM measurement).
And we've sure enjoyed watching, I can tell you.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, I hope we got all the rocks, Tony, that are here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We got all the rock types that look different from any other rock type.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, is that right on the thermal blanket?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: No, it folds down, and...Let me show you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (There's) something it attaches to down there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I got one more battery to go.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, do you want to get that (LPM reading)?
And don't walk towards the LPM there, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I'm going to read the LPM, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The center mirror on the Rover is a little streaky, but it's...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: X is 322, Y is 530, Z is 510.
Can't turn it on but 10 seconds at a clip.
X is 322, Y is 531, Z is 507.
(Pause) X is 321, Y is 531, Z is 510.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...and when you put the rock on...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...we'd like a couple of cross-Suns of it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'll get the camera for you.
And I guess I'll go on back and take the brush.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You mean you didn't dust the lens, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Dust the lens over the mirror.
Then you got to redust the mirrors.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's why I'm going to bring it around this way.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You're pointed about 10 degrees down, out to the Rover...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Those mirrors are as clean as we can dust them, Houston.
If they don't cool down, then there's a problem with thermal.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I say I'm going back (to the LM).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I promise not to get in until you dust me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's only my fifth time, I think.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Five times in how many hours is not too bad, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Long Pause) Can't get over (meaning that he is still amazed by) this big crater, John, behind us.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's still level and the Sun Shadow is still aligned.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, is your TV camera working?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'll be around to John in a minute.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To himself) Close the Hand Tool Carrier.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hey, don't forget that mag on that camera.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...of it while you were out there?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You're giving me that minute, aren't you, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I just got a picture of one of the great moments in history, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Laughing) Charlie looking down into a crater that's (laughing) 10 feet (laughing) 10 feet ...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...10 feet to the rear footpad, and 25 foot deep.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: If you'll bag that one (meaning the rock on top of the LPM)...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And the Read switch is going off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We've got it here, and you can go on in.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: What do you want...(Stops to listen) Okay.
What do you want me to do with the LPM, want to get it out of the way so it doesn't flap around and hit the LCRU at lift-off or something?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're not too worried about it, just leave it there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, John, when you bag that (rock), we'll need a bag number.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Coughs) Charlie, this is a pretty good rock.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's got a spectacular little zap pit in it.
It's lined and it's all silvery and glassy.
Fred Hörz will appreciate this rock.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You can see those sugary-textured ones.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...hustle on back in, we're getting up against it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We're getting up against the time limit.
We'd like you to get on in.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, I'm standing at the footpad ready...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's not a PLSS time limit.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...ready to get dusted and get my antenna...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's not a PLSS time limit.
That's in time for getting ready for lift-off.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Kind of looks...(Stops to listen) Oh, I don't remember.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Boy, Houston, the beauty of this place is absolutely incredible!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The pilot who missed the crater.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: To your left is that crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That'd be pretty good to miss it on landing and fall in on it before taking off, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, the way I've been falling, I probably would.
That's why I steered way clear.
(Pause) Boy, this backpack, once you get it torqued off, you can't stop it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (If) you take the camera off, I'll put the (LPM) rock in this SCB over here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Did you give them the bag number?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm up to frame count 168 on magazine E.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's not going to come out, because...(the film didn't advance?)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, you're going to have to take it off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We got to take it off.
Just shot 169 of the old Rover sitting there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, it's an incredibly good machine.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now, we got some work to do here, boy.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You ought to see your back.
I couldn't have gotten any dirtier than you.
(Pause) The only other thing we need, John, is the far UV mag.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think this stuff is just ingrained into the suit right now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I don't think we're going to be able to get it off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A little bit's coming off your arm when I whack it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Don't lose the fender off the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, you ought to see the top of your helmet.
(Pause) Looks like it's little mud drops.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Get a little farther away, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Long Pause) I'll kick off my legs (when I get up on the ladder), John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Could you close that (thigh-mounted, strap-on) pocket so the dirt...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Want to get the pockets off?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, I don't think we have time.
Let's get...I just want to get the thing (meaning the cover flap on the pocket) closed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's where all that dust came in from yesterday, was...Yours is closed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, let me try (to dust) you now.
(Pause) Uh-oh, did I turn your comm?
There we go a little bit, it's working.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Boy, I tell you, Houston, if we just had some air up here, we could plow this.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Sure is good-looking dirt, I'll tell you that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, I think I got most of that stuff off that Rover wheel because your (Static) ...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can you put your arm over your helmet?
(Static fades) Let me spend some time here.
That's probably about as good as we're gonna do, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, you know, I don't think we need to worry about the antennas, but let me get yours.
Look at the top of your...Let me worry about that; let me get the top of your PLSS cleaned off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Here, come on by the ladder.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Oh, yours is dirty, too, I couldn't reach it though; gonna bypass it.
It might be a good idea to let me...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, we're gonna keep the OPSs, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We better...You better lean over and let me get yours.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I heard of dusting off, but I didn't know we were going to have to go from the top down.
Stand up, Charlie, and let me get the back of it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You know that engine bell didn't even blow out that big old rock over there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We should be pressurized in about 5 minutes.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Boy, is that dirty up there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, spin around just a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You want me to get down?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Okay, that's the best I can do, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, boy, that's about it, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's about the best we can do.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let me...Bend over and I'll get your antenna.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: About had it and it slipped out.
Five-minute operation here (stowing John's OPS antenna).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, Houston, this portable life-support system is really a good piece of gear.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Doesn't sound like it, does it?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Why don't you go ahead and get in?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You want to get that antenna?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We don't need it anymore, do we?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I don't know what happened to the comm.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We lost you for a while.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're getting kind of back on the time line, we'd like to hustle you on in there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Charlie's climbing through the door right now, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And you've got the UV (magazine) to get yet?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: What happened to the comm, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think we had a dropout down here.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And we'll skip the track-light test; and just let you get on in.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, we've already tested it once.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm sorry, John, but I brought some dirt in with me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm going to bring up the...Houston, I'm going to reset the far UV camera.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) And the can is full, cassette is being removed.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And it and bag 6 are gonna go up the ladder this time, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Charlie, you're going to get a feedwater flag pretty soon.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To Tony) Boy, you just hit it right on the nose!
There it (meaning the feedwater tone) goes.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'll be down (meaning "behind schedule") about 10 minutes when you get in.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I forgot to wind my watch, so that's why I was asking.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Charlie, this bag is coming open.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Straining) Let me get up on the porch [and] get it in there good.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm gonna bring the ETB up now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, and you got a big rock bag on your left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, verify you took the magazine off your (Hasselblad) camera.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Boy, you got that up fast.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and the UV cassette is in the ETB?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The UV cassette is in the spacecraft.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'll go down and get the big rock bag now, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Long Pause) Well, that's the last of the old orange juice junk.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can't make the ETB (means the big rock bag) stay closed.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Have to take it up open.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It won't...There's no...There's some snaps on it, but don't worry about that.
We'll get that later if you can bring it up open.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm disconnecting the LEC and dropping it under the LM.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: After I bring this (big rock bag) in, wait 2 seconds and let me get behind the hatch and...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, that's a big rock right there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Charlie's getting behind the hatch, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: So I can get in that baby.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I'm back as far as I can get, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, let me get my visor up here...(Pause) See what I'm doing.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, you got it coming great.
You're going to have to come right a little bit...(Correcting himself) left a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's as far left as I can get, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let me get my bum out of your way.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I forgot to turn off your feedwater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: With his running out like that, I still have to get it, huh, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Muttering) "Probably a good idea." (Pause) Okay, feedwater's off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Primary Water's off; that's Closed.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, that's closed and locked, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, let me...I'll going get the...If you can scootch over just to the right just a little bit.
Let me get this dump valve.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's affirmative on the aft...(correcting himself) on the overhead.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Circuit breaker (16) ECS: Cabin Repress to Close.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Press Reg A and B to Cabin.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You got the PLSS Oxygen, Off?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And the Backroom sends a great big "Outstanding."
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: They kept us going and thinking.
So, it was a two-way street.
transcript.speaker: Duke
